summary: The Quality of Well-being Scale shows good agreement between self-report and interview formats, with similar underlying constructs even though they assess slightly different time periods. Despite methodological differences such as timing or contextual influence, both approaches reliably measure essential aspects of patient well-being due to shared construct validity.
